rationale: In the theory section, the authors do more than list this citation: they summarize Zhao and Pechmann (2007, Experiment 2) and use its result to motivate their own compatibility predictions. The cited paper provides prior evidence that promotion versus prevention message focus interacts with frame valence, which the citing paper then tests with coupon framing and time-restriction cues. Even without method or data reuse, this is a meaningful conceptual input to hypothesis development rather than a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Zhao and Pechmann's regulatory-fit finding as a transferable principle and applies it to retail sales promotions. It tests analogous compatibility effects in a new setting (shopping basket size and composition) with different cues and outcomes.
